questions.question: What is the purpose of abstract classes in Python?
keyPoints: Abstract classes provide a way to define a class that cannot be instantiated on its own and is meant to be inherited by other classes.

questions.question: What is the difference between abstract classes and interfaces in Python?
keyPoints: Abstract classes can have both abstract and concrete methods, while interfaces can only have abstract methods.

questions.question: How do you define an abstract class in Python?
keyPoints: You define an abstract class in Python by using the 'ABC' class from the 'abc' module and decorating the abstract methods with the '@abstractmethod' decorator.

questions.question: What is the purpose of interfaces in Python?
keyPoints: Interfaces provide a way to define a contract that must be implemented by any class that implements it.

questions.question: Can you instantiate an abstract class in Python?
keyPoints: No, you cannot instantiate an abstract class in Python.

questions.question: Explain the difference between abstract classes and interfaces in Python. Provide examples to illustrate your points.
keyPoints: Abstract classes can have both abstract and concrete methods, while interfaces can only have abstract methods.
Abstract classes are meant to be inherited by other classes, while interfaces are meant to be implemented by other classes.

questions.question: Explain how to define an abstract class in Python. Provide an example to illustrate your points.
keyPoints: You define an abstract class in Python by using the 'ABC' class from the 'abc' module and decorating the abstract methods with the '@abstractmethod' decorator.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of interfaces in Python. Provide an example to illustrate your points.
keyPoints: Interfaces provide a way to define a contract that must be implemented by any class that implements it.
